ui_shell_responsibilities: Renders index.html with the static page chrome: header, username input (id='username-input'), PAT input (id='pat-input', type='password'), submit button (id='lookup-button'), card container (id='card-container'), status/error region (id='status-region').

Wires submit (click on #lookup-button OR Enter key in #username-input) to: (1) read username + pat values, (2) call fetchProfilePayload(username, pat || null), (3) on success delegate payload + derivations to card-renderer, (4) on each error_type from the api-client contract render the matching status message.
Provides a labeled link to GitHub's PAT creation page (https://github.com/settings/tokens/new?description=GitHub%20Profile%20Card&scopes=) next to the PAT field (FC.3).
api_client_consumed_exports: fetchProfilePayload(username, pat) — see api-client--data-derivers.json for full signature

error_handling_mapping.ui_message: No GitHub user found with handle "{username}".
The Personal Access Token was rejected. Check the token and try again.
GitHub rate limit hit. Try again in a few minutes, or supply a PAT for a much higher limit.
Could not reach api.github.com. Check your network connection.
A GitHub Personal Access Token is required to load pinned repos and contribution data. The token field is above.

invariants: ui-shell never touches fetch() directly — all network goes through api-client.
Submitting an empty username is treated as a no-op (does not call api-client).
After a successful lookup, the form remains enabled and a second lookup updates #card-container in place (FC.10).
rationale: Single direction of dependency keeps ui-shell purely presentational with respect to network; api-client owns all HTTP semantics including error taxonomy.
